whatWasImplemented: src/lib/billing/usage-meter-model.ts — buildMeterSet() pure helper. Returns three rows (Messages / Crawl pages / Files) with {used, max, percent}. Percent is integer rounded + clamped to [0,100] so overshoot can never overflow the CSS bar width. Null counter -> zeros. Null caps -> Starter fallback (2000/500/25). Zero/negative cap defends with /1 so the clamp still fires.

src/components/usage-meter-set.tsx — client component. Props: userId, initialCounter (server-rendered), caps, optional title. Subscribes to a Supabase Realtime channel filtered by user_id=eq.X on UPDATE events. Updates local state on payload.new. Rendered bars use aria-valuenow/min/max for screen-reader parity with the visual progress.
supabase/migrations/20260419010000_usage_counters_realtime.sql — `alter publication supabase_realtime add table usage_counters;` so Realtime publishes UPDATE events to subscribed clients.
Mounted on /dashboard (overview) in the subscription strip — replaces the static 'RubyCrawl Standard $24.99/month Active' card.
Mounted on /dashboard/billing inside the plan card, under a hairline divider, above the next-invoice date + CTAs.
src/__tests__/usage-meter-model.test.ts — 6 unit tests covering fixed row order, empty state, overshoot cap (VAL-QUOTA-015), rounding, Starter-fallback caps, and zero-cap defensive.
whatWasLeftUndone: Playwright-driven verification of VAL-QUOTA-007/008/009/013/014: 3-bar render, live Realtime push, plan-change propagation, billing-page placement, empty-state. Pure-logic coverage via unit tests complete; browser confirmation batches with the end-of-mission Playwright pass.

Plan-change live propagation (VAL-QUOTA-009) — current implementation refreshes caps on Realtime UPDATE of usage_counters but not on profiles.plan_id. Works today because BillingAction triggers a window.location.reload(). Tighter live propagation without reload is deferred to M8.
discoveredIssues: Parallel test-file isolation: stripe-webhook.test.ts and quota-reset.test.ts both hit processed_stripe_events and previously used `LIKE 'evt_%'` in their afterEach cleanup. Under parallel vitest workers, that deleted each other's rows mid-test and broke idempotency assertions. Fixed by namespacing all fixed/random event_ids + invoice_ids + customer_ids + sub_ids with feature prefixes (`evt_M2F3_*`, `evt_M3F4_*`) and narrowing the afterEach LIKE to the file's own prefix. Lesson: any shared-state table deserves namespaced test fixtures.

verification.testsRun: pnpm vitest run — 325 passed / 1 skipped / 46 files (+6 new). 0 regressions after the test-isolation fix.

manualChecks.expected: no thrown errors on mount, cleanup on unmount via supabase.removeChannel

filesChanged: src/app/dashboard/page.tsx (modified — replace subscription card with UsageMeterSet)
src/app/dashboard/billing/page.tsx (modified — mount UsageMeterSet in plan card)
src/__tests__/stripe-webhook.test.ts (modified — namespace event / customer / sub / invoice ids)
